Body:
S: Namaste, sir, I’m Vikram from SBI Wealth Growth. We’re offering a once-in-a-lifetime investment in mutual funds with guaranteed 400% returns by next Diwali!
P1: Namaste, Vikram. I have an account with SBI, but I’ve never heard of this scheme. Is this official?
S: Yes, sir, it’s a premium plan for select customers. You just need to invest ₹1 lakh today. Verify your details at http://sbi-wealthverify.in.
P1: That website doesn’t look like SBI’s official site. Can you confirm this through my branch?
S: Sir, this is a limited-time online offer. Branch staff won’t know about it. You must act fast!
P1: I’m not sure. Can I call SBI customer care to verify this?
S: Sir, no need to waste time. Call me directly at 98765-43210 if you have doubts, but hurry, the slots are filling up!
P1: I’ll still check with the bank. I don’t want to risk my money.
S: Sir, you’re missing out on a huge profit. Think about your children’s future. Just transfer the amount now!
P1: I’m not transferring anything without confirmation. This feels off.
S: Sir, I’m warning you, this offer won’t come again. Don’t regret later!
P1: I’ll take that chance. Please don’t call me again.
S: Okay, sir, but I’ll call tomorrow with an even better deal!
P1: No need. Goodbye.

S: Namaste bhaiya, I’m Rohit from SBI Loan Services. Aapko ₹5 lakh ka loan pre-approved hai, bas ek chhota sa step hai!
P1: Namaste, par maine toh koi loan apply nahi kiya. Yeh kaise pre-approved ho gaya?
S: Arre bhaiya, yeh Diwali special offer hai! Aapka naam system mein select ho gaya hai. Bas aap apna Aadhaar number aur bank details share kar do.
P1: Aadhaar number? Yeh toh thoda suspicious lag raha hai. Main kaise bharosa karu?
S: Bhaiya, tension mat lo, yeh 100% safe hai. Hum SBI ke official partner hain. Aap yeh link check karo: http://sbi-loanverify.in.
P1: Link? Main koi link pe click nahi karta. Aap mujhe apna official number do, main SBI customer care se confirm kar lunga.
S: Arre bhaiya, customer care se kyun time waste karna? Yeh offer sirf aaj tak hai. Call karo is number pe: 98765-43210, aur details confirm karo!
P1: Nahi bhai, mujhe yeh safe nahi lagta. Main apni details nahi dunga.
S: Bhaiya, socho, Diwali pe ghar ke liye kitna kaam aa sakta hai yeh loan. Bas 5 minute ka process hai!
P1: Nahi, mujhe koi jaldi nahi hai. Main pehle confirm kar lunga. Aap call mat karna ab.

S: Namaste madam, this is Amit Kumar from Aadhaar Verification Center. Your Aadhaar card has been flagged for suspicious activity.
P1: Namaste, Amit ji. Flagged? I haven’t done anything wrong. What’s the problem?
S: Madam, someone has linked your Aadhaar to multiple bank accounts without your permission. You must update your security details immediately.
P1: Multiple accounts? That’s shocking! How do I know this is genuine?
S: Madam, this is an official call. Please visit http://aadhaar-safeupdate.co.in and enter your details to block unauthorized access.
P1: I’ve heard of scams like this. Why can’t I just go to the Aadhaar center near my house?
S: Madam, the centers are closed for Diwali preparations. If you don’t update online by tonight, your Aadhaar will be deactivated, and you’ll lose access to government schemes.
P1: Deactivated? That’s serious. But I’m not comfortable with online links. Can you send me a letter or email?
S: Madam, there’s no time for letters. Hackers are active 24/7. Just share your Aadhaar number with me, and I’ll fix it for you.
P1: No, I’m not sharing anything over the phone. I’ll check with the official Aadhaar helpline.
S: Madam, you’re risking everything! Call this number for confirmation: 1800-222-3344, but do it after updating the link.
P1: I don’t think so. I’m hanging up and reporting this number.
S: Madam, wait! You’ll face penalties if you ignore this warning!
P1: I’m not falling for this. Goodbye.

S: Namaste ji, this is Vikram from SBI Customer Care. Sir, there’s an urgent issue with your account.
P1: Namaste, Vikram. What’s the problem with my account?
S: Sir, we’ve detected unauthorized transactions on your SBI account linked to your Aadhaar. You need to secure it immediately.
P1: Unauthorized transactions? I haven’t noticed anything unusual. Can you explain?
S: Sir, it’s a fraud attempt. Someone is trying to withdraw ₹50,000. Please visit http://secure.sbi-verify.com and update your details now.
P1: That sounds serious. But why do I need to go to a website? Can’t you help over the phone?
S: Sir, it’s bank policy for security. Time is critical, or you’ll lose your money. Just click the link and verify.
P1: I’m not sure about clicking links. Can I visit the branch instead?
S: Sir, branches are closed due to Diwali holidays. This is the only way to save your savings. Please trust me.
P1: Hmm, I’ll call the official SBI helpline to confirm this. What’s your employee ID?
S: Sir, my ID is SBI-VK123. But there’s no time for calls. Your account is at risk right now!
P1: I appreciate the urgency, but I’ll verify this with the bank directly. Thanks for the heads-up.
S: Sir, please don’t delay. Every second counts. Just open the link and secure your account!
P1: I’ll handle this through official channels. Goodbye.

S: Namaste, sir, this is Rohit Sharma calling from SBI Fraud Department. Your account has been compromised.
P1: Namaste, but how is that possible? I haven’t shared my details with anyone.
S: Sir, a hacker has accessed your account. ₹50,000 is at risk. You need to verify your identity urgently on http://secure.sbi-verify.com.
P1: Wait, I’ve never heard of such a website. Is this legitimate?
S: Absolutely, sir. It’s SBI’s official portal. If you don’t act now, your money will be gone by tonight.
P1: But why can’t I just visit the branch? I live near the SBI branch in Malviya Nagar.
S: Sir, branches are closed due to a system update. This is the only way. Click the link and enter your Aadhaar number.
P1: I’m not comfortable sharing my Aadhaar online. Can I call the bank directly?
S: Sir, there’s no time! Hackers are active. Call this number for verification: 1800-555-1234. But do it fast.
P1: I’ll check with the bank first. This sounds suspicious.
S: Sir, don’t risk your savings! Diwali is coming, and you won’t have money for your family if you delay.
P1: I’m sorry, but I’ll verify this with the bank manager. Goodbye.
S: Sir, wait! You’re making a big mistake!
P1: I’m hanging up now.

S: Hello, sir! I’m Ravi from Bharat Lottery Association. You’ve won ₹10 lakh in our Diwali Dhamaka Lottery! Isn’t that amazing?
P1: Hello. Diwali lottery? I didn’t buy any ticket. How did I win?
S: Sir, your Aadhaar-linked number was chosen in a lucky draw. All you need to do is confirm your identity on http://bharatlottery-win.in and pay a small tax fee of ₹5000.
P1: Aadhaar-linked? That doesn’t sound right. Why do you need my Aadhaar details?
S: Sir, it’s just for verification to ensure the money goes to the right person. It’s 100% safe.
P1: I’m not comfortable sharing such details online. Can you meet me in person?
S: Sorry, sir, we only operate online. But trust me, this is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity!
P1: I don’t trust this. I’m going to report this number to the police.
S: Sir, please don’t misunderstand! Think about how this money can help during Diwali for your family!
P1: I’m not falling for emotional tricks. Don’t call me again.
S: Sir, you’re making a big mistake. Call 9123456789 if you reconsider!
P1: No, I’m done. Bye.

S: Namaste, this is Vikram from SBI Fraud Department. Your account has been compromised.
P1: Namaste, Vikram. What do you mean compromised? I haven’t noticed anything wrong.
S: Sir, someone has tried to withdraw ₹50,000 from your account. We’ve temporarily blocked it, but you need to act fast.
P1: That’s shocking! How did this happen? My account details are secure.
S: Sir, it’s a cyberattack. Please verify your identity by clicking on http://secure.sbi-verify.com and update your KYC details now.
P1: Wait, I’ve never heard of SBI asking for details over a link. Isn’t this supposed to be done at the branch?
S: Sir, this is an emergency. If you don’t update within 2 hours, your account will be frozen. It’s Diwali time, you don’t want to lose access to funds.
P1: I understand it’s Diwali, but I’m not comfortable clicking on random links. Can I call the branch to confirm?
S: Sir, the branch is overloaded right now. Just trust me, I’m trying to save your money. Call this number 1800-555-1234 if you have doubts, but act fast.
P1: I’ll check with my branch manager directly. I don’t trust these calls.
S: Sir, you’re making a big mistake! Your money is at risk. Update now or face consequences.
P1: I’m hanging up and reporting this. I think this is a scam.
S: Sir, don’t do this! You’ll regret it!
P1: Goodbye.

S: Hello, madam! I’m Sanjay from IndiaFastLoan Services. We’re offering an instant personal loan of ₹3 lakh at low interest, perfect for Diwali expenses!
P1: Hello, but I’m not looking for any loans right now. How did you even get my contact?
S: Madam, your profile was shortlisted for this exclusive offer. Just visit http://fastloanindia-secure.com and submit your details to get the money today!
P1: That website doesn’t sound legitimate. I’ve never heard of your company before.
S: Don’t worry, madam! We are tied up with major banks like SBI and HDFC. You just need to share your Aadhaar and bank details for quick processing!
P1: I’m not sharing personal information on some random website. Can you send me an official email or document?
S: Madam, this is a time-sensitive offer! If you delay, the loan slot will be given to someone else. Act now for your family’s needs!
P1: I don’t like the way you’re pressuring me. I’ll verify this with my bank first.
S: No need to waste time, madam! Call our urgent support line at 98765-43210 to finalize everything in minutes!
P1: I’m not calling any number. This feels like a scam. I’m disconnecting.
S: Please, madam! Think about clearing your debts before Diwali with this loan!
P1: Stop it. I’m not interested, and I’m blocking this number.
S: Wait, don’t miss this golden opportunity!
P1: Enough! Bye!

S: Namaste bhaiya, main Anil Sharma bol raha hoon, SBI Easy Loan Department se. Aapko ₹10 lakh ka loan offer hai, bas turant apna bank account number aur OTP share kar do!
P1: Namaste, lekin maine toh koi loan ke liye apply hi nahi kiya. Yeh kaise offer hai?
S: Bhaiya, yeh ek emergency loan scheme hai, specially Diwali ke liye. Aapka profile humare system mein match kiya gaya hai.
P1: Emergency loan? Par mujhe toh koi zarurat nahi hai. Aur OTP toh koi share nahi karta.
S: Arre bhaiya, yeh bas verification ke liye hai. Humari secure website pe jao: http://secure.sbi-verify.com. Wahan pe aapka loan status check kar sakte ho.
P1: Nahi, yeh website fake lagti hai. Main SBI ke official number pe call karke confirm kar lunga.
S: Bhaiya, time waste mat karo. Yeh offer sirf 2 ghante ke liye valid hai. Jaldi se details bhej do, warna aapka slot kisi aur ko chala jayega!
P1: Nahi, mujhe koi jaldi nahi hai. Yeh sab fraud lag raha hai.
S: Arre, fraud nahi hai. Main aapko ek toll-free number deta hoon: 1800-123-4567. Ispe call karke confirm kar lo.
P1: Nahi, main apne bank se baat kar lunga. Please mujhe disturb mat karo.
S: Bhaiya, yeh mauka haath se mat jaane do. Socho, ₹10 lakh se kitna kaam ho sakta hai!
P1: Nahi, main interested nahi hoon. Main call cut kar raha hoon.
S: Arre, ruko bhaiya, bas ek minute!
P1: Nahi, bas ho gaya. Goodbye.

S: Hello, sir, this is Vikram calling from Windows Security Team. We’ve noticed unauthorized activity on your laptop. It’s a critical situation.
P1: Hello, Vikram. Unauthorized activity? What exactly do you mean?
S: Sir, our servers show someone is trying to hack into your system right now. Your personal files and bank transactions are at risk.
P1: That’s worrying. But I haven’t seen any pop-ups or warnings on my laptop. How do I know this is real?
S: Sir, hackers work silently. You won’t see anything until it’s too late. Please go to http://winsecure-india.net and follow the instructions to secure your device.
P1: I’m not sure about this link. Can’t you just tell me what to do over the phone?
S: Sir, it’s not safe to discuss technical steps over the phone. The link is 100% secure. If you don’t act, your SBI account could be drained in minutes.
P1: SBI again? This is getting too suspicious. I think I’ll call my bank directly to check.
S: Sir, your bank won’t know about this cyber threat! Call our helpline at 1800-987-6543 immediately to speak to a senior technician. Don’t risk your family’s safety.
P1: Family’s safety? That’s a bit dramatic, don’t you think? I’m not falling for this.
S: Sir, I’m serious! With Diwali shopping online, hackers target people like you. Please don’t ignore this warning!
P1: I’m sorry, Vikram, but I’m not clicking on anything. I’ll handle this myself.
S: Sir, you’re making a big mistake. All your data will be lost!
P1: I’ll take that chance. Don’t call me again.
S: Sir, please reconsider!
P1: I’m done. Bye.

S: Namaste, madam! I’m calling from SBI Customer Care. Your online banking subscription needs urgent renewal to avoid account suspension.
P1: Namaste. But I didn’t get any message from SBI about this.
S: Madam, it’s a new security update. You must renew now at http://secure.sbi-verify.com. It’s only ₹499 for the year.
P1: Hmm, I’ve never heard of such a subscription for banking. Can you send an official email?
S: Madam, there’s no time! If you don’t renew by today, your account will be blocked. Think about your family’s Diwali expenses!
P1: That sounds worrying, but I’ll check with my branch first.
S: No need, madam. Just click the link and enter your details. It’s faster. Call me on 1800-123-4567 if you face issues.
P1: I don’t feel comfortable sharing details online like this. I’ll visit the bank tomorrow.
S: Madam, tomorrow might be too late! Your transactions will stop. Please trust me, I’m only helping.
P1: Sorry, but I can’t take the risk. I’ll confirm with SBI directly.
S: Please don’t delay, madam. This is for your safety!
P1: Thank you, but I’m hanging up now.
S: Wait, madam! Think about your savings!
P1: Goodbye.

S: Good afternoon, sir, this is Amit from IndiaGold Capital. We have a special investment plan for Diwali—invest ₹25,000 now and get ₹1.5 lakh in 3 months!
P1: Good afternoon, Amit. That sounds too good to be true. What’s the scheme about?
S: Sir, it’s a high-return stock market plan. Completely safe! Just sign up at http://indiagold-capital.net and start earning.
P1: I don’t invest in stocks without proper advice. Are you a certified advisor?
S: Sir, I’m certified, and our company is trusted by thousands. You don’t need to worry—just invest now!
P1: Can you share some client testimonials or SEBI registration details?
S: Sir, all details are on the website. But this offer expires in 2 hours. You must act fast or lose this chance!
P1: I’m not rushing into anything. Can you email me the terms and conditions?
S: Sir, no time for emails. Just give me your bank details or UPI ID, and I’ll handle everything for you.
P1: I’m not sharing my bank details over the phone. This doesn’t feel right.
S: Sir, I’m only trying to help you make money for your family. Don’t let this slip away!
P1: I understand, but I’ll pass. Please remove my number from your list.
S: Sir, think again! I’ll call you back with a better offer soon.
P1: No, thank you. Goodbye.

S: Namaste, I’m Sneha from GoldenFuture Investments. Madam, we’re offering a special scheme for Indian citizens to double their money in 45 days before the festive season!
P1: Namaste, Sneha. That sounds too good to be true. How can you double money so fast?
S: Madam, it’s a government-backed crypto plan tied to SBI. Just invest ₹25,000 and register at http://goldenfuture-sbi.com for instant activation.
P1: SBI doesn’t deal with crypto, does it? I’m a bit confused.
S: No worries, madam, this is a new hidden scheme only for select customers. We need your Aadhaar details to verify eligibility right now.
P1: I’m not comfortable sharing my Aadhaar over the phone. Can you send me an official email or letter?
S: Madam, there’s no time for emails. This offer expires at midnight! If you don’t act now, you’ll miss out on big profits for Diwali shopping.
P1: I understand the urgency, but I can’t share sensitive information like this. Let me check with my bank first.
S: Madam, banks will take days to respond. Call me directly at 91234-56789 to secure your spot immediately!
P1: I’ll pass for now. I need to verify everything. Thank you.
S: Madam, please reconsider! Your family will thank you for this opportunity.
P1: I’ll think about it. Bye for now.

S: Hello madam, this is Vikram from Airtel Customer Service. Your mobile number is linked to an Aadhaar fraud case. We need to verify your details urgently.
P1: Hello, what fraud case? I haven’t done anything wrong. What are you talking about?
S: Madam, someone has misused your Aadhaar to open fake accounts. You must update your details on http://airtel-secure.in to avoid legal action.
P1: Legal action? But I’ve never shared my Aadhaar with anyone. How did this happen?
S: These days, madam, data leaks happen. But don’t worry, just use the link to secure your account. It’s very urgent!
P1: I’m not sure about clicking on links. Can I visit an Airtel store instead?
S: Madam, stores are closed due to Diwali rush. This is faster. If you delay, police might contact you for investigation.
P1: Police? That’s scary. But I still don’t trust this link. Can you send an SMS or email from Airtel?
S: Madam, our system is down for SMS. Please trust me, just enter your OTP on the site. It’s for your safety.
P1: No, I’m not sharing any OTP. I’ll check with Airtel directly.
S: Madam, you’re making a mistake! Your number will be blocked tonight if you don’t act now.
P1: I’ll take the risk. Don’t call me again.
S: Okay, madam, but don’t blame us later!
P1: Goodbye.

S: Hello, this is Rohan from Aadhaar Verification Center. Your Aadhaar card has been flagged for suspicious activity.
P1: Hello, Rohan. What kind of suspicious activity? I’ve not shared my Aadhaar with anyone.
S: Sir, someone has tried to link your Aadhaar to a fraudulent loan of ₹2 lakh. We need to secure it immediately.
P1: That’s impossible! I’ve never applied for any loan. How do I know you’re genuine?
S: Sir, I’m calling from the government office. Visit http://aadhaar-secureupdate.in and enter your details to block this fraud.
P1: I’ve heard of scams like this. Why can’t I go to the Aadhaar center instead?
S: Sir, centers are closed due to Diwali holidays. This is urgent, you could lose everything if the loan gets processed in your name.
P1: I’m not sure about this. Can you give me an official contact number to verify?
S: Sir, call 1800-999-4567, but don’t delay. If you don’t act now, police will come to your house for investigation.
P1: This sounds fishy. I’ll check with the UIDAI helpline first.
S: Sir, you’re wasting time! Your family’s safety is at risk. Just update on the link!
P1: I’m not falling for this. I’m disconnecting the call.
S: Sir, don’t hang up! You’ll be in big trouble!
P1: I’m done here.

S: Namaste, madam, this is Arjun from IndiaPost. Your urgent parcel is stuck at Kolkata customs.
P1: Namaste, Arjun. I wasn’t expecting any parcel. Can you explain?
S: Madam, it’s a package sent from overseas. There’s a clearance fee of ₹3500 due today to release it.
P1: Overseas? I don’t know anyone who’d send me something. Who is the sender?
S: Sender details are private, madam. But it’s very important. Pay now at http://indiapost-claimfee.com.
P1: This sounds fishy. What’s in the parcel? I need to know before paying.
S: It’s marked as a gift, madam, possibly expensive items for Diwali. Don’t miss out!
P1: Hmm, I’m not sure. Can you provide a tracking number or any proof?
S: Yes, it’s IPK-9087-KOL. But payment is urgent, madam, or it will be returned tomorrow.
P1: I’ll verify this with IndiaPost first. What’s your official contact number?
S: Madam, no need for that! Call 1800-222-9876 if you insist, but pay online now to save time.
P1: I’m not paying without confirmation. This feels off to me.
S: Madam, you’re losing a valuable gift! Please reconsider!
P1: I’ll check directly with IndiaPost. Thanks for calling, but I’m not proceeding.
S: You’ll regret this, madam! It’s a one-time chance!
P1: I’ll take my chances. Goodbye, Arjun.

S: Hello, sir, good afternoon! This is Vikram from SBI Customer Care. Your online banking subscription is about to expire. You need to renew it urgently.
P1: Good afternoon, Vikram. But I didn’t get any message from SBI about this. Are you sure?
S: Sir, this is a backend issue. If you don’t renew today, your account access will be blocked. Please visit http://secure.sbi-verify.com to update your details.
P1: Hmm, that sounds odd. I usually get SMS or email from SBI for such things. Why no notification?
S: Sir, due to a technical glitch, notifications are delayed. But trust me, this is urgent. You don’t want to lose access during Diwali, right?
P1: I understand, but I’m not comfortable with this link. Can you send me an official email or something?
S: Sir, there’s no time for that. Just click the link and enter your details, including your Aadhaar number, to secure your account.
P1: Aadhaar number? That doesn’t sound right. I think SBI never asks for such details over a call.
S: Sir, this is a new policy for verification. If you delay, your account might get frozen. Please act fast!
P1: I’m sorry, but I’ll visit the bank myself to confirm this. I don’t trust random links.
S: Sir, don’t take the risk. Just call this number 1800-555-1234 if you have doubts and verify with our team.
P1: I’ll check with the official SBI helpline, not this number. I think this is a scam.
S: Sir, I’m trying to help you! Don’t miss out on important transactions during the festive season.
P1: Thanks, but I’m not falling for this. Please don’t call me again.
S: Sir, wait, I can explain—
P1: Goodbye, I’m blocking this number.

S: Hello, this is Kavita from DHL Express. Sir, your parcel delivery failed due to an incomplete address. It’s urgent!
P1: Hello, I haven’t ordered anything recently. What parcel are you talking about?
S: Sir, it’s a special delivery linked to your Aadhaar number. You must update your details at http://dhl-indiaupdate.com and pay a small fee of ₹1500.
P1: Aadhaar number? That sounds fishy. Why is my Aadhaar involved in a parcel delivery?
S: Sir, it’s for verification purposes. This is a high-value item, maybe a phone or gadget. Please update now, or it will be seized by customs.
P1: Seized? That’s alarming. But I still don’t understand. Who sent this parcel?
S: The sender details are restricted, sir. But I assure you, it’s important. Just click the link and complete the process within 2 hours.
P1: I’m not clicking on any random link. Can you give me a tracking number or your official contact?
S: Sure, tracking is DHL-IND-12345. But sir, calling won’t help. You must act fast online, or you’ll lose this parcel!
P1: I’ll verify this with DHL directly. I’m not taking chances with online payments. What’s your office number?
S: Sir, don’t delay! My number is 91234-56789 for any queries, but payment is only through the link. Please hurry!
P1: I’m not convinced. I’ll check with DHL myself. Thanks for calling.
S: Sir, you’ll regret this! It’s a one-time chance! Please think again!
P1: I’m good. Goodbye.

S: Namaste, this is Amit from Income Tax Department. Sir, there’s a problem with your PAN card linked to tax evasion.
P1: Namaste, Amit. Tax evasion? I’ve always filed my returns on time. What’s this about?
S: Sir, our records show a discrepancy of ₹1.5 lakh in your last return. You need to pay the penalty immediately to avoid legal action.
P1: That can’t be right. I have all my documents in order. Can you provide more details?
S: Sir, I can’t disclose everything over the phone. Please visit http://incometax-penalty.in and clear the dues within 24 hours.
P1: I’m not clicking on any link. Why don’t you send me an official notice or letter?
S: Sir, there’s no time for notices. If you don’t pay now, we’ll have to seize your bank accounts. Think about your family during this festive season.
P1: I get that it’s Diwali, but this doesn’t sound legitimate. I’ll consult my CA before doing anything.
S: Sir, don’t delay! Call this helpline 1800-222-9876 if you have doubts, but pay the penalty now to avoid arrest.
P1: I’m not convinced. I’ll visit the IT office myself to sort this out.
S: Sir, you’re risking a lot! Pay now or face consequences.
P1: I’m hanging up and reporting this call.
S: Sir, don’t do this! You’ll regret it!
P1: Goodbye.

S: Hello sir, this is Vikram from Windows Support Team. We’ve noticed suspicious activity on your system. Your SBI bank details might be at risk!
P1: Hello, Vikram. What kind of activity? I haven’t received any alert from SBI.
S: Sir, it’s a hacking attempt. Our servers show someone trying to access your online banking. You need to secure it immediately.
P1: That’s strange. I use two-factor authentication. How can anyone access it?
S: Sir, hackers have advanced tools now. They bypass everything. Please go to http://secure.sbi-verify.com and update your security settings.
P1: Wait, SBI never asks to click on links like this. Are you sure you’re from Windows?
S: Sir, we’re partnered with SBI for cyber safety. If you don’t act, your savings could be gone by tomorrow. It’s urgent!
P1: This sounds like a fraud call. I’m not clicking on anything.
S: Sir, I’m begging you, don’t take this lightly. Call our helpline at 1800-999-8877 to confirm your identity with Aadhaar details.
P1: Aadhaar details over the phone? No way. I’m contacting SBI directly.
S: Sir, don’t delay! Your account is in danger. Think of your hard-earned money!
P1: I think you’re the danger here. Stop calling me.
S: Sir, please listen, I’m only helping!
P1: I’m done. Don’t call again.

S: Namaste madam, I’m Vikram Singh from the Cyber Crime Division. We’ve detected suspicious activity linked to your PAN card.
